# Flyway Migrations Instructions

## Description
Governs PostgreSQL schema changes. Apply whenever a domain model or JPA
entity change requires a schema change.

## Rules
- Migrations live in `infrastructure/src/main/resources/db/migration/` (in
  the `:infrastructure` module), named
  `V<n>__<description>.sql` with a strictly incrementing integer `<n>`
  (`V1__create_customer_table.sql`, `V2__add_customer_phone.sql`, ...).
  Check the highest existing `V<n>` before creating a new file — never
  reuse or skip numbers.
- **Never edit a migration that may have already run** (i.e. anything
  already committed) — add a new migration instead, even to fix a mistake
  in a previous one. Flyway checksums applied migrations and will refuse to
  start if a shipped file changes.
- `spring.jpa.hibernate.ddl-auto=validate` is permanent — Hibernate will
  refuse to start if the JPA entity and the actual schema disagree. Every
  entity field change needs a matching migration in the same PR.
- `spring.flyway.baseline-on-migrate=true` is set for local/dev
  convenience; don't rely on it to skip writing a proper migration.

## Table Conventions (see V1__create_customer_table.sql)
- Surrogate key: `id BIGINT GENERATED BY DEFAULT AS IDENTITY PRIMARY KEY`.
- Legacy correlation key (when the resource is ingested from
  emerion-load-service): `external_id VARCHAR(64) NOT NULL` +
  `CONSTRAINT uk_<table>_external_id UNIQUE (external_id)` — this is the
  ingestion upsert key.
- Timestamps: `created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()`,
  `updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()`.
- Enums: `VARCHAR(n)` with `DEFAULT` matching the domain enum's safest
  default (e.g. `'UNKNOWN'`), validated at the application layer via
  `@Enumerated(EnumType.STRING)` — don't use a Postgres native `ENUM` type
  (harder to alter later, and Hibernate's native enum support has more
  friction than a plain varchar + app-level validation).
- Index anything used in a `WHERE`/filter from a query use case — e.g.
  `idx_customer_name` (case-insensitive `LOWER(name)`, matches the
  `LIKE LOWER(...)` query in `CustomerSpringDataRepository.search`) and
  `idx_customer_status`.

## Adding a Column
```sql
-- V2__add_customer_phone.sql
ALTER TABLE customer ADD COLUMN phone VARCHAR(32);
```
Then: add the field to `CustomerJpaEntity` (nullable unless you also
backfill), `Customer` domain model, both REST mappers, and the OpenAPI
schema.

## Testing Migrations
- `support.PostgresIntegrationTest` (Testcontainers) runs the full Flyway
  migration chain against a real Postgres on every integration test run —
  a broken migration fails the test suite, not just production. Always run
  at least one integration test locally after adding a migration.
